settings.heading: A 1,000-year-old tradition for the way we live now.
settings.lede: IONIQ began with a simple observation — modern life is depleting people faster than supplements are replenishing them. The answer wasn't another supplement. It was a salt.

settings.heading: "It's not aging. It's depletion."
settings.body:
We started where every supplement project starts — by looking at what the bestselling products on the shelf actually contained, and asking what people were trying to fix.

The fix list was the same: brain fog at 3pm, slower recovery, fragmented sleep, a dimmer energy baseline. Most people had been blaming aging. But the symptoms were responding to mineral and nootropic replenishment in weeks — not years.
settings.pullquote: Modern life is mineral-depleting at a rate the supplement industry has not caught up to.
settings.caption: Fig. 01 · The current stack

settings.heading: We went to Korea looking for jugyeom.
settings.body:
The salt is the formula. Every other electrolyte brand starts with the same industrial sea salt — same source, same mineral profile, same ceiling on performance.

The answer was 1,000 years old, in a small Korean kiln, with a tradition called jugyeom — bamboo salt. Sea salt packed into bamboo stalks, sealed with clay, fired at 1,500°C — nine times.

The result: pH 12, 70+ trace minerals, and uniquely among foods — hydroxyl ions that support cellular antioxidant activity.
settings.caption: Fig. 02 · 9× fire-roasted

settings.heading: One sachet. Four supplement types.
settings.body:
Once we had the salt, the rest of the formula wrote itself. 750mg sodium for cellular hydration. 200mg potassium for muscle. 50mg magnesium for recovery. Cognizin®, L-tyrosine, Rhodiola for focus. ALCAR + taurine for mitochondrial energy. Hydroxyl ions from bamboo salt for antioxidant activity.

Every dose is on the label. No proprietary blends. No "performance complex."
settings.caption: Fig. 03 · 4 categories, 1 sachet

settings.heading: How IONIQ got here.
settings.copy: Two years from the first founding memo to the first box shipped.

settings.title: The founding memo
settings.body: The original observation: people are buying four supplements to chase symptoms that aren't getting better.

settings.title: The first salt trial
settings.body: We tested 23 commercial salts — none could anchor a real formula.

settings.title: First trip to Korea
settings.body: Six weeks visiting traditional bamboo-salt kilns. Came back convinced 9× was the only product.

settings.title: Formulation locked
settings.body: 14+ ingredients across four supplement categories. Every dose clinical. Every milligram on the label.

settings.title: Clinical pilot — n=24
settings.body: Independent lab measured plasma volume, RPE and recovery in 24 athletes. 64% hydration improvement vs water.

settings.title: First box ships
settings.body: IONIQ Bamboo Salt Elixir — Lemon — ships from our Brooklyn warehouse. Sold out in three weeks.

settings.title: New flavours
settings.body: Watermelon and Blueberry launch. Sour Mango drops in Q4.

settings.heading: The standards behind the sachet.
settings.copy: Six things we decided at the start that we haven't changed since.

settings.title: Every dose on the label
settings.body: No proprietary blends. No "performance complex." If you can't see the dose, you can't trust it.

settings.title: Clinical doses only
settings.body: If a study used 250mg, we put 250mg in. Not 50mg with a famous brand name.

settings.title: Real food, not flavouring
settings.body: Real lemon. Real fruit when we expand. No "natural flavours," no artificial colours.

settings.title: Tested, every batch
settings.body: Heavy metals, mineral content, banned substances. ICP-MS verified. Lab reports on request.

settings.title: Subscription you can leave
settings.body: Skip, pause, cancel any time from your dashboard. No phone calls. No retention emails.

settings.title: The 60-day guarantee
settings.body: If it doesn't work, we refund the box. Even if you've used it. We'd rather lose the order than your trust.

settings.heading: Who's behind it.
settings.copy: A small team — formulation scientists, athletes and operators — building IONIQ from a single warehouse in Brooklyn.

settings.name: Min-Jae Park
settings.bio: Spent six years in Korean food-science research before starting IONIQ. The trip to the bamboo kilns was, in retrospect, inevitable.

settings.name: Dr. Anya Mehta
settings.bio: Exercise physiologist with a PhD in cellular hydration. Designed the formula and runs the clinical trials.

settings.name: Joren Cull
settings.bio: Lives between Brooklyn and the Korean kilns. Has personally inspected every batch of bamboo salt that's ever shipped.

settings.heading: Try IONIQ for 60 days.
settings.copy: If it doesn't fix what depletion did, send the box back. We refund the whole thing, no questions, no friction.
